title: Rescue StandardError
description: Catch a potential error using the begin/rescue block to prevent program termination.
problemStatement:
Safety first! Use a `begin` and `rescue` block to catch a zero division error (`10 / 0`). Inside the `rescue` block, print `"Divided by zero!"`.

**Requirement:** The program should not crash when run.
hints: Ruby uses 'rescue' instead of the 'catch' keyword found in other languages.
solutionCode:
begin
  10 / 0
rescue ZeroDivisionError
  puts "Divided by zero!"
end
